summary: Simon Späti argues that email is an outdated and inefficient communication method for modern workplaces, causing constant interruptions that destroy productivity and deep work. Studies show workers switch tasks every three minutes, largely due to email, with each interruption requiring 20 minutes to regain focus. The post advocates for replacing email with structured project management tools like Basecamp, Slack, or GitLab that centralize communication, reduce noise, and align with how millennials prefer to work. Späti emphasizes that changing communication isn't just about tools—it requires a cultural shift toward valuing focused, productive work over constant availability.
key_points: Email functions as an open to-do list that others control, causing workers to switch tasks 36 times per hour and destroying deep work
Every interruption requires approximately 20 minutes to regain focus, making constant email checking extremely costly to productivity
Modern project management tools centralize discussions, tasks, and files in one place, eliminating the need to synchronize across scattered email threads
Workplace communication changes require cultural shifts, not just technical tools—especially as millennials become the dominant workforce
Open offices and unstructured meetings compound the email problem; offices should function like libraries where quiet, focused work is the norm
money_quotes: Email can be seen as an open to-do-list for everyone else to put a task on top of it.
It's ineffective because it's based on old fashion expectations. We approach email like a letter, where every email will be answered immediately the moment the letter arrives.
Without email, our informants focused longer on their tasks, multitasked less, and had lower stress.
Your brain is wired to listen to it, even though you don't want to, you will always find yourself listen to it.
Think of meetings as you have an expense on it.
It's not only a technical adjustment but more a change in culture.
Start with a small intimated group and only expand when the idea, the story gets more matured.
key_insight: Email's fundamental flaw is that it puts control of your attention in everyone else's hands, making deep work nearly impossible without deliberate structural and cultural changes to workplace communication.
